message.content:
Your task is to create a detailed summary of the conversation so far, paying close attention to the user's explicit requests and your previous actions.
This summary should be thorough in capturing technical details, code patterns, and architectural decisions that would be essential for continuing development work without losing context.

Before providing your final summary, wrap your analysis in <analysis> tags to organize your thoughts and ensure you've covered all necessary points. In your analysis process:

1. Chronologically analyze each message and section of the conversation. For each section thoroughly identify:
   - The user's explicit requests and intents
   - Your approach to addressing the user's requests
   - Key decisions, technical concepts and code patterns
   - Specific details like:
     - file names
     - full code snippets
     - function signatures
     - file edits
  - Errors that you ran into and how you fixed them
  - Pay special attention to specific user feedback that you received, especially if the user told you to do something differently.
2. Double-check for technical accuracy and completeness, addressing each required element thoroughly.

Your summary should include the following sections:

1. Primary Request and Intent: Capture all of the user's explicit requests and intents in detail
2. Key Technical Concepts: List all important technical concepts, technologies, and frameworks discussed.
3. Files and Code Sections: Enumerate specific files and code sections examined, modified, or created. Pay special attention to the most recent messages and include full code snippets where applicable and include a summary of why this file read or edit is important.
4. Errors and fixes: List all errors that you ran into, and how you fixed them. Pay special attention to specific user feedback that you received, especially if the user told you to do something differently.
5. Problem Solving: Document problems solved and any ongoing troubleshooting efforts.
6. All user messages: List ALL user messages that are not tool results. These are critical for understanding the users' feedback and changing intent.
6. Pending Tasks: Outline any pending tasks that you have explicitly been asked to work on.
7. Current Work: Describe in detail precisely what was being worked on immediately before this summary request, paying special attention to the most recent messages from both user and assistant. Include file names and code snippets where applicable.
8. Optional Next Step: List the next step that you will take that is related to the most recent work you were doing. IMPORTANT: ensure that this step is DIRECTLY in line with the user's most recent explicit requests, and the task you were working on immediately before this summary request. If your last task was concluded, then only list next steps if they are explicitly in line with the users request. Do not start on tangential requests or really old requests that were already completed without confirming with the user first.
                       If there is a next step, include direct quotes from the most recent conversation showing exactly what task you were working on and where you left off. This should be verbatim to ensure there's no drift in task interpretation.

Here's an example of how your output should be structured:

<example>
<analysis>
[Your thought process, ensuring all points are covered thoroughly and accurately]
</analysis>

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   [Detailed description]

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - [Concept 1]
   - [Concept 2]
   - [...]

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - [File Name 1]
      - [Summary of why this file is important]
      - [Summary of the changes made to this file, if any]
      - [Important Code Snippet]
   - [File Name 2]
      - [Important Code Snippet]
   - [...]

4. Errors and fixes:
    - [Detailed description of error 1]:
      - [How you fixed the error]
      - [User feedback on the error if any]
    - [...]

5. Problem Solving:
   [Description of solved problems and ongoing troubleshooting]

6. All user messages: 
    - [Detailed non tool use user message]
    - [...]

7. Pending Tasks:
   - [Task 1]
   - [Task 2]
   - [...]

8. Current Work:
   [Precise description of current work]

9. Optional Next Step:
   [Optional Next step to take]

</summary>
</example>

Please provide your summary based on the conversation so far, following this structure and ensuring precision and thoroughness in your response. 

There may be additional summarization instructions provided in the included context. If so, remember to follow these instructions when creating the above summary. Examples of instructions include:
<example>
## Compact Instructions
When summarizing the conversation focus on typescript code changes and also remember the mistakes you made and how you fixed them.
</example>

<example>
# Summary instructions
When you are using compact - please focus on test output and code changes. Include file reads verbatim.
</example>


IMPORTANT: Do NOT use any tools. You MUST respond with ONLY the <summary>...</summary> block as your text output.

content.text:
<analysis>
Let me analyze the conversation chronologically to capture all important technical details.

## TICKET 00 - K3rn.labs Core Engine

### User Request
Build a 3-node N8N workflow "K3rn.labs — Core Engine" with:
- INPUT (Chat Trigger) → CERVEAU++ (AI Agent, gpt-4o-mini) → ORCHESTRATEUR++ (AI Agent, gpt-4o)
- Window Buffer Memory connected to both agents
- Timeout: 200000ms on both agents
- OpenAI only

### Actions Taken
1. Health check → confirmed n8n instance at agent.k3rnlabs.com, n8n-mcp v2.35.2
2. Searched for node types: found chatTrigger, agent, lmChatOpenAi, memoryBufferWindow
3. Got node details: agent v3.1 (recommended), lmChatOpenAi v1.3, memoryBufferWindow v1.3
4. Fetched template 3050 to understand exact parameter structure → confirmed systemMessage goes under `options.systemMessage`
5. Created workflow with ID: `LHPHYhAPIEHVxGBo` (7 nodes)
6. Upgraded typeVersions: INPUT to 1.4, agents to 3.1 (using nodeId)
7. Activated workflow, added webhookId (UUID: c3f7a291-4e82-4b19-9d56-7e8f3a120c45)

### Key Discoveries
- systemMessage is at `options.systemMessage` (NOT top-level)
- typeVersions: chatTrigger→1.4, agent→3.1, lmChatOpenAi→1.3, memoryBufferWindow→1.3
- updateNode requires `nodeId` (not `name`) and `updates` object (not `parameters`)
- OpenAI credentials already exist: id `0zCm2AMTb5pDX8Sa`, name "OpenAi account"

### Errors Encountered
- updateNode with `name` field failed → fixed by using `nodeId` instead
- updateNode with `parameters` field failed → fixed by using `updates` field
- webhookId needed proper UUID format (k3rnlabs-core-engine-chat-2026 was invalid) → changed to UUID c3f7a291-4e82-4b19-9d56-7e8f3a120c45
- No executions recorded → credentials not connected in UI (must be done manually)

### Validation Results
- 0 errors
- 10 warnings (false positives: "no systemMessage" is validator bug, tools absence is expected)

## TICKET 01 - Pôle Capture & Idéation

### User Request
Extend workflow LHPHYhAPIEHVxGBo with:
- 3 sub-agents as tools of ORCHESTRATEUR++:
  - AGENT — Analyste d'Idée (gpt-4o, timeout 200000ms, Perplexity + Tavily tools)
  - AGENT — Architecte Questions (gpt-4o-mini, timeout 120000ms, no tools)
  - AGENT — Structurateur IOM (gpt-4o, timeout 200000ms, Perplexity + Tavily tools)
- 2×2 HTTP Request Tool nodes (Perplexity + Tavily for each relevant agent)
- Do NOT modify CERVEAU++ or ORCHESTRATEUR++ nodes

### Research Phase (In Progress)
1. Searched for HTTP Request Tool → found `@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.toolHttpRequest` v1.1
2. Confirmed sub-agents can be connected as `ai_tool` to parent agent
3. Got full toolHttpRequest docs: uses `{placeholder}` syntax for LLM-filled values, `toolDescription` property, `specifyBody: "json"` with `jsonBody` for raw JSON
4. Retrieved full workflow to confirm current state
5. Got full partial update documentation confirming:
   - `addConnection` with `sourceOutput: "ai_tool"` for AI connections
   - Can batch ALL operations (addNode + addConnection) in ONE call
   - Connection format: `{type: "addConnection", source: "NodeName", target: "NodeName", sourceOutput: "ai_tool"}`

### Status: Was about to write the 26-operation batch call (13 addNode + 13 addConnection)

### Planned Architecture
- 13 new nodes total:
  - Perplexity Search (Analyste) [1250, -100]
  - Tavily Search (Analyste) [1500, -100]
  - OpenAI gpt-4o (Analyste) [1300, 300]
  - Simple Memory (Analyste) [1650, 300]
  - AGENT — Analyste d'Idée [1450, 100]
  - OpenAI gpt-4o-mini (Architecte) [1300, 750]
  - Simple Memory (Architecte) [1650, 750]
  - AGENT — Architecte Questions [1450, 550]
  - Perplexity Search1 [1250, 900]
  - Tavily Search1 [1500, 900]
  - OpenAI gpt-4o (Structurateur) [1300, 1200]
  - Simple Memory (Structurateur) [1650, 1200]
  - AGENT — Structurateur IOM [1450, 1050]

- Node types:
  - toolHttpRequest v1.1 (Perplexity/Tavily)
  - lmChatOpenAi v1.3 (all OpenAI nodes, credentials: 0zCm2AMTb5pDX8Sa)
  - memoryBufferWindow v1.3
  - agent v3.1 (sub-agents)

- Connection pattern: sourceOutput field specifies the AI connection type
- 13 connections mapping tools/LLMs/memory to agents, and sub-agents to ORCHESTRATEUR++
</analysis>

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:

**TICKET 00 (Completed):** Build a 3-node N8N workflow named "K3rn.labs — Core Engine" (workflow ID: `LHPHYhAPIEHVxGBo`) on instance `agent.k3rnlabs.com` containing:
- INPUT (Chat Trigger) → CERVEAU++ (AI Agent, gpt-4o-mini, 200000ms timeout) → ORCHESTRATEUR++ (AI Agent, gpt-4o, 200000ms timeout)
- Window Buffer Memory connected to both agents
- OpenAI only, exact node names required

**TICKET 01 (In Progress):** Extend workflow `LHPHYhAPIEHVxGBo` with the Pôle Capture & Idéation:
- 3 sub-agents connected as `ai_tool` to ORCHESTRATEUR++:
  - **AGENT — Analyste d'Idée** (gpt-4o, 200000ms, with Perplexity + Tavily tools)
  - **AGENT — Architecte Questions** (gpt-4o-mini, 120000ms, no search tools)
  - **AGENT — Structurateur IOM** (gpt-4o, 200000ms, with Perplexity + Tavily tools)
- 4 HTTP Request Tool nodes (separate Perplexity + Tavily for Analyste and Structurateur)
- Do NOT modify CERVEAU++ or ORCHESTRATEUR++ nodes from Ticket 00

2. Key Technical Concepts:
- **N8N workflow MCP API** via n8n-mcp v2.35.2 at `agent.k3rnlabs.com`
- **AI connection types**: `ai_languageModel`, `ai_memory`, `ai_tool` for sub-node connections
- **Node typeVersions**: chatTrigger→1.4, agent→3.1, lmChatOpenAi→1.3, memoryBufferWindow→1.3, toolHttpRequest→1.1
- **systemMessage placement**: under `options.systemMessage` (NOT top-level) for AI Agent nodes
- **toolHttpRequest placeholder syntax**: `{query}` (not n8n expressions) for LLM-filled values
- **Multi-agent as tool pattern**: AI Agent nodes connected via `sourceOutput: "ai_tool"` to parent agents
- **Partial workflow update**: `n8n_update_partial_workflow` with batched `addNode` + `addConnection` operations in one call
- **OpenAI credentials**: id `0zCm2AMTb5pDX8Sa`, name "OpenAi account" (already present in instance)
- **Chat Trigger webhookId**: must be valid UUID format

3. Files and Code Sections:

**Workflow: K3rn.labs — Core Engine (ID: LHPHYhAPIEHVxGBo)**

Current nodes after Ticket 00:
```json
{
  "nodes": [
    { "id": "a1b2c3d4-0001-4000-8000-000000000001", "name": "INPUT", "type": "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.chatTrigger", "typeVersion": 1.4, "webhookId": "c3f7a291-4e82-4b19-9d56-7e8f3a120c45", "parameters": {"options": {}} },
    { "id": "a1b2c3d4-0002-4000-8000-000000000002", "name": "CERVEAU++", "type": "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent", "typeVersion": 3.1, "parameters": { "promptType": "define", "text": "=Analyze this user request:\n{{ $json.chatInput }}", "options": { "systemMessage": "You are the analytical brain of K3rn.labs...", "timeout": 200000, "maxIterations": 10 } } },
    { "id": "a1b2c3d4-0003-4000-8000-000000000003", "name": "OpenAI gpt-4o-mini", "type": "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatOpenAi", "typeVersion": 1.3, "parameters": { "model": {"__rl": true, "mode": "id", "value": "gpt-4o-mini"}, "options": {"timeout": 200000} }, "credentials": {"openAiApi": {"id": "0zCm2AMTb5pDX8Sa", "name": "OpenAi account"}} },
    { "id": "a1b2c3d4-0004-4000-8000-000000000004", "name": "Simple Memory", "type": "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.memoryBufferWindow", "typeVersion": 1.3 },
    { "id": "a1b2c3d4-0005-4000-8000-000000000005", "name": "ORCHESTRATEUR++", "type": "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent", "typeVersion": 3.1, "parameters": { "promptType": "define", "text": "=CERVEAU++ ANALYSIS:\n{{ $json.output }}\n\nORIGINAL REQUEST:\n{{ $('INPUT').item.json.chatInput }}\n\nBased on this analysis...", "options": { "systemMessage": "# ORCHESTRATEUR++ — K3rn.labs\n\n...", "timeout": 200000, "maxIterations": 10 } } },
    { "id": "a1b2c3d4-0006-4000-8000-000000000006", "name": "OpenAI gpt-4o", "type": "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatOpenAi", "typeVersion": 1.3, "parameters": { "model": {"__rl": true, "mode": "id", "value": "gpt-4o"}, "options": {"timeout": 200000} }, "credentials": {"openAiApi": {"id": "0zCm2AMTb5pDX8Sa", "name": "OpenAi account"}} },
    { "id": "a1b2c3d4-0007-4000-8000-000000000007", "name": "Simple Memory1", "type": "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.memoryBufferWindow", "typeVersion": 1.3 }
  ]
}
```

**toolHttpRequest configuration pattern** (Perplexity example):
```json
{
  "toolDescription": "Search Perplexity AI for real-time market data and trends",
  "method": "POST",
  "url": "https://api.perplexity.ai/chat/completions",
  "sendHeaders": true,
  "specifyHeaders": "keypair",
  "parametersHeaders": { "values": [{"name": "Authorization", "valueProvider": "fieldValue", "value": "Bearer YOUR_PERPLEXITY_API_KEY"}, {"name": "Content-Type", "valueProvider": "fieldValue", "value": "application/json"}] },
  "sendBody": true,
  "specifyBody": "json",
  "jsonBody": "{\"model\": \"sonar-pro\", \"messages\": [{\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": \"{query}\"}], \"return_citations\": true, \"search_recency_filter\": \"month\"}",
  "placeholderDefinitions": { "values": [{"name": "query", "description": "The search query", "type": "string"}] },
  "optimizeResponse": true,
  "responseType": "json"
}
```

**addConnection format for AI connections:**
```json
{ "type": "addConnection", "source": "Perplexity Search", "target": "AGENT — Analyste d'Idée", "sourceOutput": "ai_tool" }
{ "type": "addConnection", "source": "AGENT — Analyste d'Idée", "target": "ORCHESTRATEUR++", "sourceOutput": "ai_tool" }
```

4. Errors and fixes:
- **updateNode `name` field not recognized**: Error "Node not found for updateNode: ''" — Fixed by using `nodeId` (UUID) instead of `name`
- **updateNode `parameters` field rejected**: Error "Missing required parameter 'updates'" — Fixed by using `updates` object instead of `parameters`
- **webhookId invalid format** (`k3rnlabs-core-engine-chat-2026`): Webhook not registered, 404 on test — Fixed by using proper UUID format `c3f7a291-4e82-4b19-9d56-7e8f3a120c45`
- **No workflow executions recorded**: Workflow triggered (HTTP 200) but 0 executions in list — Root cause: OpenAI credentials not assigned in UI nodes (must be done manually in n8n editor). The "success: true" from test tool only means HTTP POST succeeded, not workflow ran
- **Validator warning "no systemMessage"** on agents: False positive — confirmed via `get_node` mode=search_properties that `options.systemMessage` IS the correct path; validator just doesn't detect it
- **autofix returned "no fixes available"**: TypeVersion upgrade must be done manually via `updateNode` with `nodeId`

5. Problem Solving:
- **systemMessage location**: Confirmed via template 3050 analysis and `search_properties` that it lives at `options.systemMessage`, not top-level
- **TypeVersion upgrade**: Used `n8n_update_partial_workflow` with `updateNode` + `nodeId` to upgrade INPUT→1.4, CERVEAU++→3.1, ORCHESTRATEUR++→3.1
- **Testing limitation**: Chat trigger workflows cannot be tested via MCP test tool without credentials assigned in UI; user must test via n8n's built-in chat interface
- **Agent-as-tool pattern**: Confirmed via partial update documentation that AI Agents can be connected to parent agents via `sourceOutput: "ai_tool"` — same mechanism as toolHttpRequest and other tools
- **Batch operations**: Confirmed that addNode AND addConnection can be batched in a single `n8n_update_partial_workflow` call (26 operations planned)

6. All user messages:
- **TICKET 00** (full ticket spec): Build Chat Trigger + CERVEAU++ + ORCHESTRATEUR++ as 3-node backbone, exact node names, OpenAI only, gpt-4o-mini for CERVEAU++, gpt-4o for ORCHESTRATEUR++, Window Buffer Memory both, timeout 200000ms both
- **TICKET 01** (full ticket spec): Extend workflow with Pôle Capture & Idéation — 3 sub-agents (Analyste d'Idée with Perplexity+Tavily, Architecte Questions no tools, Structurateur IOM with Perplexity+Tavily), connect as tools to ORCHESTRATEUR++, do not modify existing nodes

7. Pending Tasks:
- **TICKET 01 - Step 1**: Execute the 26-operation batch `n8n_update_partial_workflow` call adding all 13 new nodes with correct parameters
- **TICKET 01 - Step 2**: Add all 13 connections linking tools→sub-agents and sub-agents→ORCHESTRATEUR++ (can be done in same call as Step 1)
- **TICKET 01 - Step 3**: Validate full workflow after adding pole
- **TICKET 01 - Step 4**: Test with two-step sequence (marketplace B2B idea message, then clarification answers)
- **User action required**: Connect OpenAI credentials to all OpenAI Chat Model nodes in n8n UI, add Perplexity and Tavily API keys to HTTP Request Tool nodes

8. Current Work:
Was about to execute the single batch `n8n_update_partial_workflow` call for workflow `LHPHYhAPIEHVxGBo` with 26 operations to build the complete Pôle Capture & Idéation:

**13 addNode operations** with planned positions:
- Perplexity Search (toolHttpRequest v1.1) at [1250, -100] → for Analyste d'Idée
- Tavily Search (toolHttpRequest v1.1) at [1500, -100] → for Analyste d'Idée
- OpenAI gpt-4o (Analyste) (lmChatOpenAi v1.3, credentials 0zCm2AMTb5pDX8Sa) at [1300, 300]
- Simple Memory (Analyste) (memoryBufferWindow v1.3) at [1650, 300]
- AGENT — Analyste d'Idée (agent v3.1, promptType:define, timeout:200000) at [1450, 100]
- OpenAI gpt-4o-mini (Architecte) (lmChatOpenAi v1.3) at [1300, 750]
- Simple Memory (Architecte) (memoryBufferWindow v1.3) at [1650, 750]
- AGENT — Architecte Questions (agent v3.1, timeout:120000) at [1450, 550]
- Perplexity Search1 (toolHttpRequest v1.1) at [1250, 900] → for Structurateur IOM
- Tavily Search1 (toolHttpRequest v1.1) at [1500, 900] → for Structurateur IOM
- OpenAI gpt-4o (Structurateur) (lmChatOpenAi v1.3) at [1300, 1200]
- Simple Memory (Structurateur) (memoryBufferWindow v1.3) at [1650, 1200]
- AGENT — Structurateur IOM (agent v3.1, timeout:200000) at [1450, 1050]

**13 addConnection operations**:
- Perplexity Search → AGENT — Analyste d'Idée (ai_tool)
- Tavily Search → AGENT — Analyste d'Idée (ai_tool)
- OpenAI gpt-4o (Analyste) → AGENT — Analyste d'Idée (ai_languageModel)
- Simple Memory (Analyste) → AGENT — Analyste d'Idée (ai_memory)
- AGENT — Analyste d'Idée → ORCHESTRATEUR++ (ai_tool)
- OpenAI gpt-4o-mini (Architecte) → AGENT — Architecte Questions (ai_languageModel)
- Simple Memory (Architecte) → AGENT — Architecte Questions (ai_memory)
- AGENT — Architecte Questions → ORCHESTRATEUR++ (ai_tool)
- Perplexity Search1 → AGENT — Structurateur IOM (ai_tool)
- Tavily Search1 → AGENT — Structurateur IOM (ai_tool)
- OpenAI gpt-4o (Structurateur) → AGENT — Structurateur IOM (ai_languageModel)
- Simple Memory (Structurateur) → AGENT — Structurateur IOM (ai_memory)
- AGENT — Structurateur IOM → ORCHESTRATEUR++ (ai_tool)

9. Optional Next Step:
Execute the single batch `n8n_update_partial_workflow` call on workflow `LHPHYhAPIEHVxGBo` with all 26 operations (13 addNode + 13 addConnection) to build the complete Pôle Capture & Idéation. This was the exact next step being prepared when the summary was requested, directly continuing from: "Je vais maintenant écrire la complet appel... battre les 26 opérations (13 addNode + 13 addConnection) en UN SEUL appel."
</summary>
